title: Trace The Documented Communication Path Between OptiSweep OTNet And UPS Network
summary: Use this source-backed reference procedure to interpret how the training material describes communication between the OptiSweep/WCS environment on the OT network and UPS systems. The source states that the OT network and UPS network are separate, and that communication to UPS Chat XLE crosses that boundary through S-Tunnel as a secure or encrypted channel for order or sortable destination information.
when_to_use: Use when a user needs to understand or restate the documented architecture shown in the training source for how OptiSweep or WCS communicates across the OTNet and UPS network boundary to reach Chat XLE through S-Tunnel.
not_for: Do not use for actual connectivity validation.
Do not use for network configuration review or implementation.
Do not use for encryption detail review, key handling, port identification, or protocol configuration.
Do not add unsupported network settings, keys, ports, or configuration steps.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Interpret and communicate the source-stated architecture exactly as documented in the training material.

safety_notes: This is a reference-only interpretation aid, not an execution or change procedure.
Do not invent network configuration files, key handling steps, or protocol settings beyond the source statements.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the training video segment or extracted slide
Source transcript describing OT net, UPS network, Chat XLE, and S-Tunnel

access_required: Access to the training source segment training_video_day1_segment_0009
system_preconditions: The user is reviewing documented architecture only.

visual_references.description: Training slide showing OptiSweep software components and communication path labels including Chat XLE and S-Tunnel.

steps.title: Open the software and communications training segment
steps.purpose: Locate the exact source material that documents the communication boundary and named components.
steps.instruction: Open the training segment covering software and network communications and locate the references to OT net, UPS network, Chat XLE, and S-Tunnel.
steps.expected_result: The relevant training segment and supporting slide are available for review.
steps.healthy_condition: The reviewer can see the software slide and transcript evidence naming OptiSweep software, Chat XLE, and S-Tunnel.
steps.failure_condition: The reviewer cannot locate the referenced segment or artifact showing the communication labels.

actions.label: Open training segment
actions.exact_action: Open the training segment covering software and network communications and locate the references to OT net, UPS network, Chat XLE, and S-Tunnel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the software slide labels identifying Chat XLE for UPS communications and S-Tunnel for encrypted connectivity from OTNet to UPS.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the source segment or slide cannot be accessed.
Stop if a user requests operational validation rather than source interpretation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Software slide lists OptiSweep software, AVEVA - HMI, Ignition - OPC Server, Chat XLE - UPS communications, and S-Tunnel - Encryption from OTNet to UPS Corp.

steps.title: Confirm OTNet is separate from the UPS network
steps.purpose: Establish the documented network separation before tracing the communication path.
steps.instruction: Identify from the transcript that the OptiSweep environment operates on an OT network that is separate from the UPS local or corporate network.
steps.expected_result: The reviewer confirms that the source describes the OptiSweep/WCS environment as separate from the UPS network.
steps.healthy_condition: The source clearly indicates OTNet and the UPS network are separate.
steps.failure_condition: The reviewer cannot confirm the separation from the source evidence.

actions.label: Confirm network separation
actions.exact_action: Identify from the transcript that the OptiSweep environment operates on an OT network that is separate from the UPS local or corporate network.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the slide and aligned transcript context describing OTNet and the UPS network as separate environments.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the user needs proof beyond the training source.
Stop if the request shifts to actual network topology verification.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OptiSweep operates on OT net separate from the UPS network; WCS talks to UPS Chat XLE through S-Tunnel as a secure channel to get order and destination information.
OptiSweep and related control assets operate on OTNet, separate from the UPS network.

steps.title: Note the source describes the networks as separate islands
steps.purpose: Capture the source framing of the network boundary as a conceptual separation.
steps.instruction: Note that the source describes the OT network and UPS network as separate 'islands.'
steps.expected_result: The reviewer records that the source frames the OT and UPS networks as distinct separated domains.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpretation preserves the source concept of separation without adding technical detail.
steps.failure_condition: The interpretation adds unsupported assumptions about how the networks are connected internally.

actions.label: Record separation concept
actions.exact_action: Note that the source describes the OT network and UPS network as separate 'islands.'

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the user asks for unsupported details about routing, segmentation, or controls between the networks.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The training explanation presents the OT network and UPS network as separate environments and a boundary-crossing communication path through S-Tunnel.

steps.title: Identify the information needed from UPS Chat XLE
steps.purpose: Clarify why communication crosses the network boundary according to the source.
steps.instruction: Identify that the WCS or OptiSweep software needs information from UPS Chat XLE about order or sortable destination information.
steps.expected_result: The reviewer can state the source-backed reason for communication with Chat XLE.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpretation stays limited to order or destination information described by the source.
steps.failure_condition: The interpretation expands into unsupported message formats, APIs, or transaction details.

actions.label: Identify information purpose
actions.exact_action: Identify that the WCS or OptiSweep software needs information from UPS Chat XLE about order or sortable destination information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Review the Chat XLE label and transcript summary describing UPS communications and order or destination information.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if message content, interface specification, or transaction troubleshooting is requested.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: WCS talks to UPS Chat XLE through S-Tunnel as a secure channel to get order and destination information.

steps.title: Identify S-Tunnel as the documented boundary-crossing channel

steps.failure_condition: The reviewer adds unsupported details such as keys, ports, certificates, or setup steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: This is a procedural reference candidate for interpreting documented architecture, not a connectivity test or configuration procedure.
The source explains the communication boundary and secure channel concept but does not provide executable troubleshooting or setup steps.

success_criteria: The user can describe that OptiSweep or WCS operates on OTNet separate from the UPS network.
The user can state that communication to UPS Chat XLE crosses the boundary through S-Tunnel.
The interpretation remains limited to source-backed architecture statements.
healthy_conditions: OTNet and the UPS network are described as separate environments.
Chat XLE is identified as the UPS communications endpoint named in the source.
S-Tunnel is identified as the secure or encrypted channel from OTNet to UPS.
failure_conditions: The reviewer cannot locate the source segment or artifact supporting the architecture statement.
The interpretation includes unsupported details such as ports, keys, certificates, or configuration steps.
The user expects live validation or implementation guidance from this reference.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if actual connectivity validation is required, because the source provides architecture explanation only.
Escalate if configuration review is required, because the source does not provide executable setup steps.
Escalate if encryption implementation details are required, because the source only states that S-Tunnel is a secure or encrypted channel.
